commentary_text: The market assigns Brazil a 58% win probability versus the model's 39%, a 19-percentage-point gap favoring the favorite. The model sees Morocco and the draw as near-equal threats, while the market dramatically discounts Morocco to 17%. This sharpening of conviction around a Brazil victory could reflect recent form, injury status, or lineup changes unavailable to the model at its last update—factors that real-money markets absorb continuously. The dashboard surfaces this as a signal of information asymmetry, not a judgment on which assessment is more reliable.
